description: The entity communicates with customers, regulators, partners, and other external parties regarding control-related matters through defined workflows, contact channels, and timing expectations.

subcriteria.name: external_notification_triggers_and_workflow
subcriteria.reference_text: A procedure or external communications document explains when external communication is required and what type of event triggers it. Real evidence often mentions confirmed breach, service disruption, customer inquiry, regulatory notice, questionnaire response, responsible disclosure, or external notice workflow.

subcriteria.name: contact_channels_and_public_security_information
subcriteria.reference_text: The document identifies specific channels external parties can use to receive information or ask security questions. Real evidence often includes a trust center, security mailbox, support chat, status page, customer contact path, or responsible disclosure page.

subcriteria.name: notification_timing_and_coordination
subcriteria.reference_text: A strong external communication artifact includes target timing and coordinating functions. Real evidence often says customers notified within 72 hours, vendors must provide 48-hour notice, templates reviewed quarterly, legal or compliance coordinates notice, or regulators notified per applicable requirements.

subcriteria.name: third_party_security_commitments
subcriteria.reference_text: External communication evidence may also include security commitments made through contracts or public-facing materials. Real evidence often mentions DPAs, MSAs, audit rights, breach-notification obligations, trust-center security commitments, subprocessors, or published system descriptions.

mismatch_signals.reason: Internal-only personnel communication is not the same as external communication evidence.
Access-management evidence is not external communication evidence.
